content.full_text:
January 14, 1991

Jonathan Rothman
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Calif. 95804

Subject: Request for Opinion on Possible Conflict

Dear Jonathan,

I am writing to request an opinion as to whether two of my Directors have a conflict which would prohibit their participation in the following matter pending before the District Board. MCSD is considering purchase through condemnation of a 78 acre parcel of land for a community park. The owner of the parcel has indicated his intent to apply to Humboldt County to subdivide the property for residential development. If the owner proceeds with the subdivision MCSD will supply sewer and water service to the development.

Directors Joe Walund and Edward Estes both live within 2500 feet of the parcel but more than 300 feet away from the parcel. Both Directors own and live in their respective residences. Single family homes in this neighborhood currently sell for $150,000 to 200,000. Approximately, 150 residences housing approximately 400 people live within the area more than 300 feet and less than 2500 feet of the parcel proposed for purchase/development.

Persuant to section 18702.3 of the Regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission, does either Director have a disabling conflict related to their ownership interest in real property indirectly involved in the decision should MCSD consider either condemnation or extension of service.

Sincerely,

Bruce S. Buel
Manager

CC: Board
Ron Angell
